Summarized reasoning:  
Claim: Topical steroids are most effective in treating eczematous dermatitis due to its strong inflammatory pathology and direct responsiveness to topical anti-inflammatory therapy.  
Evidence: • Eczematous dermatitis involves significant skin inflammation driven by immune activation, making it highly responsive to topical corticosteroids that suppress inflammatory cytokines. • In clinical practice, topical steroids provide rapid symptomatic relief (e.g., redness, pruritus) and are considered first-line therapy for moderate-to-severe cases.  
Answer: <B>
